stories.headline: Aid groups urge swift action to avert ‘atrocities’ in Sudan’s el-Obeid
stories.summary: The city is at risk of an 'imminent ground offensive' by the RSF paramilitary and its allies, 38 NGOs warn.

stories.headline: Ukraine decimates Russian logistics, bringing chaos to Crimea
stories.summary: Kyiv circumvents air defences to target oil supplies, power stations, convoys and bridges, starving Russian front line.

stories.headline: Rainbow flags await Egypt and Iran at awkward World Cup Pride Match
stories.summary: Iran's coach refuses to discuss pride celebrations in Seattle, where his country face Egypt in a crucial Group G match.

stories.headline: South Korea to train half a million military personnel to become ‘drone warriors’
stories.summary: All branches of the military will be taught how to use technology that has become a ‘game changer on the battlefield’, says defence ministerAll of South Korea’s military forces will be trained as drone operators in a sweeping overhaul of its warfare strategy, the defence minister has said.“All soldiers should be able to use drones like a second personal firearm,” Ahn Gyu-back, who heads the defence ministry in Seoul, said on Friday. Continue read

stories.headline: UK June heat record broken again, France postpones Pride and Poland warns of wildfires as heatwave grips Europe – live
stories.summary: Estimated 150 million people in Europe could experience temperatures above 35C todayEuropean heatwave is worst ever and impossible without climate crisis, scientists sayOver in the UK, firefighters are still trying to bring a large wildfire in Derbyshire under control.The blaze, which has burned over 500 square metres of moorland and woodland on Tintwistle Moor, near Glossop, broke out on Wednesday evening, with fire crews from Manchester and Der

stories.headline: Watch: South Korea holds first live-fire drill against drone swarms
stories.summary: South Korea's air force carried out its first live-fire exercise against a simulated swarm drone attack. Military forces used Vulcan air-defense guns, a portable laser and shotguns to intercept all 50 drones during the drill.

stories.headline: 'Marketing is more important than ever': Serviceplan CEO at European CMO of the Year awards
stories.summary: Speaking to Euronews, Serviceplan Group CEO Florian Haller said marketing has evolved far beyond traditional advertising, and now sits at the centre of the "whole customer journey" where "data, technology and creativity" meet.

stories.headline: Christmas market attacker jailed for life for murdering six in Germany
stories.summary: A nine-year-old and five women were killed when Taleb Al-Abdulmohsen drove into the market in 2024.

stories.headline: US sanctions Rwanda gold refinery accused of smuggling DR Congo's minerals
stories.summary: The US alleges Gasabo Gold Refinery smuggled at least 60kg of gold in early 2026.

stories.headline: Blackwater founder Erik Prince: Trump was ‘badly advised’ on Iran
stories.summary: The mercenary-in-chief on facing public anger — and the role that private contractors will play in the future of war

stories.headline: Moscow gets a taste of Putin’s war
stories.summary: Ukrainian drones force residents in Russian capital to seek shelter for first time

stories.headline: US Goods Trade Deficit Widens to Biggest in More Than a Year
stories.summary: The US merchandise-trade deficit widened in May to the biggest in more than a year as exports pulled back from record levels and imports continued a steady march higher.

stories.headline: Bitcoin Bottom Hunters Fear Fresh Pain After $1.3 Trillion Rout
stories.summary: Bitcoin’s collapse is forcing crypto veterans to confront the question every bear market eventually asks: when does mass panic create a buying opportunity? The answer, according to many of the investors and analysts who have lived through previous boom-and-bust cycles, is: not yet.

stories.headline: Hungary to Meet Euro Entry Criteria by 2030, Premier Magyar Says
stories.summary: Hungarian Prime Minister Peter Magyar said his country could meet the European Union’s economic criteria for adopting the euro by 2030, setting out the clearest timetable yet for a path toward the common currency.

stories.headline: Trump finds partner in supreme court in his war against immigration
stories.summary: Recent rulings have bolstered president’s pitiless campaign against people of colour fleeing violence and disasterIn the US supreme court, it seems Donald Trump has found loyalists in his crusade against immigration and immigrants.In a pair of rulings on Thursday, the court allowed the Trump administration to end humanitarian protections that have granted people from Haiti and Syria the right to live and work legally in the US for more than a dec

stories.headline: Who has profited most from the war on Iran?
stories.summary: Defence contractors, energy companies and investment banks saw profits soar as war and uncertainty upended markets.

stories.headline: Indonesia risks deeper power crisis from coal supply failures
The AI cold war needs a nonalignment movement
Hormuz tanker traffic climbs to 25% of prewar level
Tokyo warehouse district emerges as testing ground for self-driving tech
Life sentence for Magdeburg attacker
stories.summary: When he drove a car into the Magdeburg Christmas market, he killed six people and injured more than 300. Taleb al Abdulmohsen has now been sentenced to life imprisonment.

stories.headline: Reflecting Pool liner cut with sharp knife or razor, National Park Service says
stories.summary: Earlier in the week Donald Trump blamed vandals for "a 300 foot long gash" in the pool and accused someone of putting fertiliser in the water.

stories.headline: Canada eligible to join Eurovision competition
stories.summary: Prime Minister Mark Carney raised the idea of Canada joining the song contest in his 2025 budget.

stories.headline: Asia stock markets slide as tech shares slump
stories.summary: Trading on South Korea's Kospi index was halted for the third time this week to prevent panic selling.

stories.headline: Housing board votes to freeze New York rents in victory for Mamdani
stories.summary: The decision was welcomed by many tenants - but landlords fear it will worsen housing conditions.

stories.headline: Row over alleged theft of donations from India's landmark Ram temple
stories.summary: Questions are being raised over the handling of cash, valuable jewellery, gold and silver offered by devotees.

stories.headline: Would farmers and fishermen back Brexit today?
stories.summary: Polls suggested the majority of farmers and fishers in England voted to leave the EU 10 years ago.

stories.headline: Volkswagen to axe up to 100,000 jobs in sweeping cost-cutting drive
stories.summary: Restructuring would remove close to one in six workers and rank among biggest corporate lay-offs of all time

stories.headline: The shrinking arguments for degrowth
stories.summary: Economic growth isn’t everything for everyone, but it turns out it’s pretty close

stories.headline: Oil Heads for Weekly Decline Despite Hormuz Ship Attack
stories.summary: Oil resumed its decline and headed for the biggest weekly drop in a month after transits through the Strait of Hormuz accelerated following an attack on a cargo ship. Tyler Kendall reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: US Natural Gas Rises on Hot Forecasts as July Contract Expires
stories.summary: US natural gas futures increased in the final day of trading for the July contract as forecasts continued to show above-average temperatures in the first week of July. Individual transactions moved the market more amid a drop-off in liquidity near expiry.

stories.headline: Aluminum Heads for Fourth Weekly Drop on Mideast Supply Return
stories.summary: Aluminum headed for a fourth weekly decline, the longest losing streak since April 2025, as a renewed selloff in tech stocks added to pressure from the dollar’s recent rally and returning Middle East supplies.

stories.headline: Oil Prices Might Keep Falling Into 2027, Strategist Amoroso Says
stories.summary: Anastasia Amoroso, chief investment strategist at Partners Group, says the concerns about higher oil prices did not play out. Speaking with Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Open Interest," Amoroso also says she expects energy prices to keep falling going into 2027. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Denmark’s EIFO Invests €200 Million in EU Fund for Tech Scale-Up
stories.summary: Denmark’s Export and Investment Fund has committed €200 million to the €5 billion Scaleup Europe Fund, becoming the only state-backed anchor investor in a vehicle designed to help build Europe’s next generation of technology champions.

stories.headline: Nigeria Draws First Tranche of $5 Billion Swap With UAE Bank
stories.summary: Nigeria has accessed the first tranche of a $5 billion derivatives deal with United Arab Emirates’ largest lender, pressing ahead with a transaction that has been scrutinized for being opaque.

stories.headline: Outrage as woman jailed for three years after criticising Somali government online
stories.summary: Sentencing of 27-year-old Sadia Moalim Ali condemned by former president and prime ministers as well as rights groupsA rickshaw driver in Somalia has been sentenced to three years in prison for comments she made on social media, in a case that has caught the public’s attention and provoked outrage in the country.Sadia Moalim Ali, a 27-year-old nursing graduate, was originally charged with insulting government institutions and incitement to commit

stories.headline: Venezuela death toll doubles as interim president vows to save ‘as many people as possible’
stories.summary: Delcy Rodríguez says foreign rescue teams are arriving but people are angry at official response and limited resourcesVenezuela’s interim president, Delcy Rodríguez, has vowed to fight to save “as many people as possible” as the official death toll from the country’s worst earthquake in more than a century almost doubled, but frustration was growing at the perceived sluggishness of the government’s response.Rodríguez said on Friday that the offic

stories.headline: Socceroos fans celebrate at city sites – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closedGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastAlbanese says Stefanovic saga ‘unfortunate’, but ‘words do matter’The prime minister was asked by the ABC about Karl Stefanovic’s departure from Nine. He had this to say:It’s a matter, of course, for Channel 9, but words do matter, and what people who are very prominent in our public discourse do, and how they conduct themselves, matters as well.And it’s unfortunate

stories.headline: Matthew Guy demands Moira Deeming apologise for assault claim after Victoria police find ‘no offence detected’
stories.summary: ‘There was no ambiguity. I did not do what was alleged,’ Guy says of fellow state Liberal MP’s accusationFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastVictorian Liberal MP Matthew Guy is demanding an apology from his colleague Moira Deeming after police closed their investigation into an allegation he assaulted her, finding there was “no offence detected”.A visibly emotional Guy a

stories.headline: Indonesian miner Merdeka Gold taps Hong Kong market with $310m listing
Bangkok governor vote highlights city's struggle to stay competitive
SoftBank shares slip over 12% on OpenAI IPO delay concerns
Vietnam accelerates biofuel push as oil risks rise and concerns mount
Chinese firms brace for new EU rules as trade deficit tops $1bn a day
India's small and mid-cap stocks draw growth-hunting investors
Poland's Tusk calls for 'mutual respect' during row with Ukraine
stories.summary: Ukraine's Volodmyr Zelensky skips major conference aimed at rebuilding his country after being stripped of a Polish state honour.

stories.headline: Trump asks Congress for billions for Iran war, after tension with Republicans
stories.summary: But the budget faces an uphill battle as the president spars with some members of his own party over the issue.

stories.headline: What Are the Paths Forward for the Housing Bill After Trump Refused to Sign It?
stories.summary: President Trump abruptly scrapped plans to sign a major housing bill. It could still become law, with or without a presidential signature. But Mr. Trump could also try to kill it.

stories.headline: Families lay flowers on barbed wire barricade on anniversary of deadly Kenya protests
stories.summary: Protesters demand justice for the dozens killed during the 2024 demonstrations and last year's anniversary protests.

stories.headline: OpenAI Leans Toward Waiting Until 2027 for IPO, NYT Says
stories.summary: OpenAI is leaning toward holding off on an initial public offering until 2027, the New York Times reported, citing three people involved in the company’s deliberations.

stories.headline: Johnson Says He Will Send Housing Bill to Trump
stories.summary: After meeting with the president, the speaker said he would send him a housing bill that Mr. Trump declined to sign this week. There was no word on whether he would sign it.

stories.headline: John Stockwell, Who Wrote a Tell-All Book About the C.I.A., Dies at 88
stories.summary: A former covert operative, he published his resignation letter in The Washington Post and went on to write “In Search of Enemies,” a book the agency sought to suppress.

stories.headline: ‘Constitutional coup’ claims as Zimbabwe senate approves extending presidential term
stories.summary: Opposition figures fear changes will further tighten 83-year-old president Emmerson Mnangagwa’s hold on powerZimbabwe is on the brink of amending its constitution to give the president more time in office, a change that the government says will bring stability but that opponents have labelled a “constitutional coup”.The upper house of Zimbabwe’s parliament voted on Wednesday 75-4 in favour of the constitutional amendments, which will allow Presid

stories.headline: New evidence casts doubt on RFK Jr testimony before Senate
stories.summary: Kennedy repeatedly said 2019 Samoa trip had ‘nothing to do with vaccines’. An email from his then colleague says they were on a vaccine-related ‘mission’New evidence has emerged that Robert F Kennedy Jr was on a vaccine-related “mission” when he visited Samoa ahead of a deadly measles outbreak in 2019, raising further questions about whether the US health secretary lied to the US Senate when he said the trip had “nothing to do with vaccines”.Reco

stories.headline: Beaten and starved: Shock in India as police rescue men from bonded labour
stories.summary: Bonded labour was outlawed in India in 1975, but it still continues in villages and small towns.

stories.headline: Stocks Waver at End of Bruising Week for AI Trade: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Another burst of selling pummeled chipmakers, whipsawing stocks once again amid concerns over whether vast investments in artificial intelligence will justify the industry’s hefty valuations.

stories.headline: UPS is losing ground to FedEx
stories.summary: The two logistics giants have similar strategies—and face a common threat

stories.headline: UN nuclear chief says inspectors will visit Iran sites as part of war deal
stories.summary: Rafael Grossi says the IAEA is "working on modalities" but an Iranian  minister says such access would only be part of a final deal with the US.

stories.headline: White House seeks additional $88 billion from Congress for Iran war, other priorities
stories.summary: The request mostly includes money for the Pentagon, with $67 billion going toward replenishing the military’s stocks of munitions and the cost of sending so many forces to the Middle East.

stories.headline: Trump abruptly cancels signing of bipartisan bill on affordable housing
stories.summary: The president posted on social media that the signing was off until the Senate passes his election integrity bill. It is unclear whether he intends to kill the housing bill.

stories.headline: Biggest city in Crimea without power after Ukraine strikes
stories.summary: Sevastopol's Moscow-installed governor warns there will be no electricity in some areas until the evening.

stories.headline: Colombia's left-wing presidential candidate concedes defeat
stories.summary: Senator Iván Cepeda was beaten by right-wing businessman Abelardo de la Espriella by less than a percentage point.

stories.headline: Silicon Valley has much to learn from the spreadsheet jockeys it despises
stories.summary: Even if financial markets disagree

stories.headline: UN says it will evacuate sailors stranded in Strait of Hormuz, as Rubio warns against tolls
stories.summary: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio warned Iran that no country can charge fees for ships to travel through the strait.

stories.headline: Dozens of ships head through Strait of Hormuz after US-Iran deal
stories.summary: Traffic in the waterway has risen since the US and Iran signed a deal aimed at ending the war, including 42 ships on Saturday alone.

stories.headline: Iran says no new commitments on nuclear sites after Vance says inspectors to be invited back
stories.summary: Iran's foreign ministry says it made "no new commitments" on nuclear inspections after talks in Switzerland.

stories.headline: Leading Pakistan activist given life sentence over soldier's killing at rally
stories.summary: Human rights campaigner Mahrang Baloch denied inciting protesters before the official's death.

stories.headline: Dettol apologises after ad to clean up 'toxic men' backfires in China
stories.summary: The ad sparked accusations of sexism in China, after featuring a man looking for a partner who is "not tainted by other men".

stories.headline: Australia's coal and gas exports violate our human rights, group says in new UN case
stories.summary: The group says that it is unlawful for Australia to continue approving fossil fuel exports without protecting its citizens.

stories.headline: Trump anticipates better relationship with Colombia under new leader
stories.summary: Abelardo de la Espriella, who preliminary results suggest is Colombia's next president, had Trump's endorsement.

stories.headline: At least 13 killed and dozens injured after Qatar gas explosion
stories.summary: The city's main liquified natural gas (LNG) processing site suffered a "technical accident" in the Ras Laffan industrial zone.

stories.headline: 'I witnessed Maradona's Hand of God' - a goal still talked about 40 years on
stories.summary: One BBC journalist's first football game had one of the sport's most controversial moments and also one of the most celebrated.

stories.headline: Fuel sales halted in occupied Crimea as Ukraine targets oil facilities
stories.summary: Fuel had already been rationed due to shortages caused by Kyiv's attacks against supply routes in Russian-occupied territories.

stories.headline: The BTS fans losing thousands as scammers cash in on comeback tour ticket war
stories.summary: As ticket demand outstrips supply by a ratio of 15 to one, desperate fans have become easy targets.

stories.headline: Currency crash and visa crackdowns force Indian students to rethink studying abroad
stories.summary: A weaker rupee and tougher immigration rules are pushing Indian students away from popular destinations like the US and UK.

stories.headline: Israeli strikes kill six people in Gaza including Al Jazeera cameraman, officials say
stories.summary: The Israeli military accused Ahmed Wishah of being a "Hamas sniper operative", without providing evidence.

stories.headline: Israel and Hezbollah continue strikes despite ceasefire agreement
stories.summary: At least 20 people have reportedly been killed by Israeli air strikes as the Israeli military said Hezbollah had fired at Israeli troops in southern Lebanon.

stories.headline: Israel and Hezbollah agree ceasefire, US says, as more Lebanon strikes reported
stories.summary: The agreement followed concerns that continued clashes would undermine the deal to end the war between the US and Iran.

stories.headline: US-Iran talks postponed as Israel launches deadly strikes in Lebanon
stories.summary: JD Vance has not travelled to Switzerland as the Israeli military said it was targeting Hezbollah and that four of its own soldiers had been killed.

stories.headline: Thousands killed in US-Israeli war on Iran - but experts say true total may never be known
stories.summary: Experts say internet, media and government restrictions have all hampered casualty reporting across the region.

stories.headline: Meet Merlin the Duck: Mexico's World Cup 'ambassador'
stories.summary: The BBC’s Will Grant was amongst the crowds swarming to meet Merlin, a pet duck who went viral for waddling around Mexico City in the Mexican team's jersey.
